home.hero_statement: Explore interactive features and virtual labs with Aurum through an inspiring learning journey.

journey.title: Structured learning path
journey.desc: From grade 8 to grade 12, each stop opens a clear and memorable chemistry topic.
journey.desc_long: From grade 8 to grade 12, Aurum guides learners through atoms, reactions, solutions, organic chemistry, and everyday applications. Each stage uses Vietnam-inspired visuals to make concepts easier to remember.

lab.title: Virtual chemistry lab
lab.desc: Observe changes, mix solutions, and test ideas in a clear simulation environment.
lab.desc_long: The virtual lab helps students practice reactions, identify substances, and observe changes safely. Simulations are designed to support classroom lessons and encourage experimental thinking.
lab.image_alt: A safe chemistry lab in a Vietnamese classroom

arena.title: Knowledge arena
arena.desc: Answer quickly, score points, and sharpen your reflexes with leveled chemistry questions.
arena.desc_long: Aurum turns practice into a friendly knowledge competition. Learners can test speed, accuracy, and formula application through each challenge round.
arena.image_alt: A friendly chemistry knowledge contest between Vietnamese students

library.title: Chemistry reference hub
library.desc: Study materials, the periodic table, and visual resources help learners review quickly.

map.title: Learning map
map.desc: Track progress on a stylized Vietnam map, unlock topics, and record your learning milestones.
map.image_alt: A stylized map of Vietnam with chemistry learning stages

lessons.title: Visual lessons
lessons.desc: Chemistry theory is presented with visuals, real-life examples, and short interactions.
lessons.image_alt: A visual chemistry lesson in Vietnamese folk-art style

leaderboard.empty: The leaderboard is waiting for the first learners...

step1.title: Build the foundation
step1.desc: Study theory through visual explanations and familiar examples.

step2.title: Practice in the virtual lab
step2.desc: Observe reactions, test hypotheses, and draw conclusions.

step3.title: Compete with knowledge
step3.desc: Train speed, accuracy, and progress through each challenge.

testimonials.title: What learners and teachers say about Aurum

reviews.name: Minh Anh
reviews.content: The illustrated lessons make reactions easier to understand, so chemistry exercises feel less intimidating.

reviews.name: Gia Bao
reviews.content: The virtual lab lets me test different observations before doing practical work in class.

reviews.name: Ms. Lan
reviews.content: Aurum works well for short review tasks, and students can follow their progress more independently.

final_cta.title: Ready to begin your chemistry journey?
final_cta.subtitle: Sign in to follow a personal learning path, save your progress, and keep exploring every day.

footer.brand_desc: Learn Chemistry through interactive games and realistic virtual experiments. A modern technology learning platform for the future generation.

classroom.title: Start your <1/><2> Chemistry</2> journey
classroom.subtitle: Each grade is a new learning stage, guiding you from foundations to real chemistry applications.

knowledge_tree.title: Complete Chemistry Knowledge Tree
knowledge_tree.subtitle: Systematize chemistry knowledge from basics to advanced topics.
knowledge_tree.instruction: Tap each lesson to start practicing.

8.title: Chemistry Foundations (Grade 8)
8.desc: Get familiar with substances, lab tools, and the first observable chemistry phenomena.

9.title: Reaction Explorer (Grade 9)
9.desc: Explore reactions, solutions, and substance transformations through safe experiments.

10.title: Atomic Structure (Grade 10)
10.desc: Study atoms, bonds, periodic trends, and the core rules behind chemistry.

11.title: Organic Chemistry (Grade 11)
11.desc: Explore carbon chains, functional groups, and compounds connected to everyday life.

12.title: Applied Chemistry (Grade 12)
12.output: Master electrochemistry, polymers, metals, and practical exam-ready applications.
12.desc: Master electrochemistry, polymers, metals, and practical exam-ready applications.

lectures.subtitle: Look up chemistry theory and video questions by grade level
lectures.no_results: No chemistry materials found matching the questions

about.title: Igniting a passion for <1/><2> Chemistry</2> in young learners
about.description: Aurum is an interactive learning journey that helps students explore atoms, bonds, and reactions. Chemistry becomes easier to understand when it can be observed and practiced safely.

safety.title: Safe Chemistry Making
safety.desc: 3D virtual labs help students practice brewing dangerous chemical with zero risk of blowing up the classroom.

gamification.title: Questions Ranks & Learning Badges
gamification.desc: Integrated gamification system with learning points and learners ranks makes learning highly engaging.

visualization.title: Arcane Vision
visualization.desc: All abstract chemical bonds and atomic structures are visualized in stunning interactive 3D.

team_section.title: Grand Council of Aurum Learning Hub
team_section.description: We bring together passionate Chemistry Masters and professional software architects. With the mantra 'Digitalizing education is the key to the future', we constantly refine every formula and lab simulation.

contact.title: Contact the <1/> Founder Council
contact.description: We are always happy to receive feedback messages, error reports, or collaboration requests. Reach out to us through our official channels below.

form.title: Draft an Message Mail
form.message_placeholder: What would you like to discuss with the council...

terms.title: Terms & <1/> Privacy Policy

acceptance.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
acceptance.p1: By accessing and using Aurum Chemistry Odyssey, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, please refrain from using our services.
acceptance.p2: We reserve the right to modify or update these terms at any time without prior notice. Your continued use of the platform after changes are posted constitutes your acceptance of the new terms.

accounts.title: 2. User Accounts
accounts.p1: To access certain features, you may need to register for an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account information and password. All activities that occur under your account are your responsibility.
accounts.p2: Sharing accounts, using cheating tools in the arena, or intentionally disrupting the community is strictly prohibited. We reserve the right to suspend accounts that violate these rules without notice.

intellectual.title: 3. Intellectual Property
intellectual.p1: All content on Aurum, including but not limited to lectures, 3D simulations, images, and source code, is owned by Aurum Chemistry Learning Hub or its licensors.
intellectual.p2: Any unauthorized copying, distribution, or commercialization of the content without our prior written consent is strictly prohibited and may result in legal action.

privacy.title: 4. Privacy Policy
privacy.p1: We are committed to protecting your personal information. Collected data (such as name, email, and learning progress) is used solely to personalize your learning experience and improve our service quality.
privacy.p2: We never sell or share your personal information with third parties for marketing purposes without your explicit consent.

liability.title: 5. Limitation of Liability
liability.p1: Lab simulations and knowledge provided on this platform are for educational and reference purposes only. Users should not perform dangerous experiments in real life without expert supervision.
liability.p2: We are not liable for any direct or indirect damages resulting from the use of information or incorrect practice of instructions found on the platform.

terms.footer_question: Have questions about the terms?<1/>Please <contact>Contact Us</contact>.

quick_guide.title: Quick Lookup
quick_guide.desc: Click elements to view 3D atomic chemistry simulations

H.name: Hydrogen
H.desc: The lightest and most abundant element in the universe. A primary component of water.
facts: Makes up 75% of the universe's mass
Solar energy comes from hydrogen fusion
Can become metallic at extreme pressure

He.name: Helium
He.desc: Inert gas, second lightest after Hydrogen. Discovered on the Sun before being found on Earth.
facts: Discovered on the Sun before Earth
Name from Helios - Greek sun god

Li.name: Lithium
Li.desc: Lightest alkali metal, soft and silvery. Reacts strongly with water.

Be.name: Beryllium
Be.desc: Light alkaline earth metal with high hardness and melting point.
facts: Does not react with water at room temp

B.name: Boron
B.desc: Hard, black metalloid, semiconductor. Essential for plants.
facts: Primary component of Pyrex heat-resistant glass
Pure boron is very hard, second only to diamond
Can absorb neutrons in nuclear reactors

C.name: Carbon
C.desc: Basic element of life, can form over 10 million different compounds.
facts: Diamond and graphite are both pure carbon
Basis of all life on Earth

N.name: Nitrogen
N.desc: Gas making up 78% of Earth's atmosphere. Essential for protein and DNA.
facts: N≡N triple bond is very stable
Used for cooling and food preservation
Essential for DNA, RNA, and protein

O.name: Oxygen
O.desc: Essential for respiration and combustion. Third most abundant element in the universe.

F.name: Fluorine
F.desc: Most reactive halogen, pale yellow gas, extremely toxic and corrosive.
facts: Reacts with almost everything, including noble gases

Ne.name: Neon
Ne.desc: Noble gas, colorless, odorless, used in neon signs.
facts: Creates signature orange-red light in neon lamps
Fifth most abundant noble gas in the universe
Does not react with any element

Na.name: Sodium
Na.desc: Soft metal, reacts strongly with water creating a yellow flame.

Mg.name: Magnesium
Mg.desc: Light metal, burns brightly with a blinding white flame.
facts: Essential for over 300 enzymes in the body

Al.name: Aluminum
Al.desc: Light metal, corrosion-resistant, widely used in daily life.
facts: Most abundant metal in Earth's crust
Aluminum cans can be recycled infinitely
Washington Monument cap is made of aluminum

Si.name: Silicon
Si.desc: Metalloid, 2nd most abundant element in Earth's crust, basis of electronic technology.
facts: Silicon Valley is named after it

P.name: Phosphorus
P.desc: Nonmetal with multiple allotropes, essential for DNA and ATP.
facts: White phosphorus glows in the dark
Essential for DNA, RNA and ATP
Red phosphorus is safe, used in matches

S.name: Sulfur
S.desc: Yellow nonmetal, smells like rotten eggs when combined with hydrogen.
facts: Rotten egg smell is H₂S containing sulfur
Used for sulfuric acid - #1 industrial chemical
Essential for methionine and cysteine amino acids

Cl.name: Chlorine
Cl.desc: Greenish-yellow gas, toxic, pungent odor. Used for water disinfection.
facts: Used as chemical weapon in WWI
Signature pungent odor like swimming pools

Ar.name: Argon
Ar.desc: Noble gas making up 0.93% of air, used in welding and light bulbs.
facts: Used in bulbs to prevent filament evaporation

K.name: Potassium
K.desc: Soft alkali metal, reacts violently with water.
facts: More explosive reaction with water than sodium
Can be cut with a knife due to softness
Essential for nerve and muscle function

Ca.name: Calcium
Ca.desc: Alkaline earth metal, primary component of bones and teeth.
facts: Makes up 1.5% of human body (bones, teeth)
Primary component of limestone and marble
Calcium metal can be cut with a knife

Fe.name: Iron
Fe.desc: Most common and important metal for industry and biology.
facts: Earth's core is mostly molten iron

Cu.name: Copper
Cu.desc: Reddish metal, second best conductor of electricity and heat after silver.
facts: Statue of Liberty is coated in copper

Zn.name: Zinc
Zn.desc: Bluish-grey metal, used for galvanizing iron against rust and in batteries.

Ag.name: Silver
Ag.desc: Precious metal with the highest electrical and thermal conductivity.

Au.name: Gold
Au.desc: Most ductile and malleable precious metal, does not tarnish.
facts: One ounce can be drawn into 50 miles of wire
Basis of global financial systems for centuries

header.desc_logged_in: Complete the storylines to become a true master chemistry.
header.desc_guest: A journey to conquer chemistry from basic to advanced for new learners from grade 8 to 12.

status.empty_title_guest: Please select a grade to show chemistry lessons
status.empty_desc: Explore the chemistry world of chemistry right now

8.title: Part 1
8.name: The Awakening Beginning
8.story: Enter the world of substances and atoms, where chemistry begins. Your mission is to understand matter and the language of formulas.

9.title: Part 2
9.name: The Transformation Journey
9.story: Reactions and visible changes reveal how elements and compounds interact.

10.title: Part 3
10.name: The Macro Leap
10.story: Go deeper into kinetics, equilibrium, and periodic structure while connecting concepts to real chemistry problems.

11.title: Part 4
11.name: The Organic Matrix
11.story: Lost in the labyrinth of Carbon atoms. Here, life forms from complex and mysterious circuits. You must find the rules that create all life.

12.title: Part 5
12.name: Reaching Evolution Peak
12.story: The final stage of high-school chemistry. Overcome electrochemistry, polymers, and materials to prepare for real applications.
